item.title: About
item.description:
I am Armstrong Yan, a backend and platform engineer based in Singapore. I care about systems that are understandable under pressure: clear interfaces, reliable operations, useful automation, and engineering practices that make teams faster without making the software harder to reason about.

This site is where I keep public notes on backend architecture, platform work, distributed systems, developer workflows, and the knowledge systems I use to connect ideas over time.

item.title: Now
item.description:
Current focus:

  • Writing more often from engineering notes instead of waiting for polished essays.
  • Improving the Obsidian-to-Hugo publishing flow for this site.
  • Studying reliable backend systems, platform abstractions, and operational habits that scale with a team.

Updated: 2026-05-06.

item.title: Projects
item.description:
Selected projects and experiments. This page stays intentionally short; each entry should point to something useful, runnable, or worth revisiting.

Current Link to heading

  • Personal website: Hugo, GitHub Pages, and an Obsidian publishing workflow for public technical notes.
  • Knowledge workflows: Small tools and templates for turning private notes into publishable writing.

Areas Link to heading

  • Backend services and platform engineering
  • Distributed systems notes and experiments
  • Developer automation and personal knowledge systems

item.title: Resume
item.description:
Profile Link to heading

Backend and platform engineer focused on reliable systems, clear engineering workflows, and practical automation.

Focus Areas Link to heading

  • Backend architecture
  • Platform engineering
  • Distributed systems
  • Cloud infrastructure
  • Developer productivity

Technology Link to heading

  • Go, Python
  • HTTP APIs, services, queues, data pipelines
  • Cloud platforms, observability, automation

Contact Link to heading

For now, the best starting point is this site and the writing published here.
